title: NarrationResponse
description: Standard turn response from the LLM. Used for Zod validation on the backend and can be passed to OpenAI structured outputs.

narration.description: The story text narrated to the player. Flowing prose, 50-100 words, optimized for TTS. No bullet points, no lists, no markdown formatting.

stateUpdates.description: Game state changes resulting from this turn. Only include fields that changed. Omit fields with no change.

healthDelta.description: Change in HP. Negative for damage (e.g. -5), positive for healing (e.g. 3). Omit if no change.

inventoryAdd.description: Items added to the player's inventory this turn. Omit if none.

inventoryRemove.description: Items removed from the player's inventory this turn. Must match existing item names exactly. Omit if none.

goldDelta.description: Change in gold. Positive for gain, negative for spending. Omit if no change.

locationChange.description: New location name if the player moved. Use short descriptive names (e.g. 'Dark Forest', 'Village Square'). Omit if no movement.

questProgress.description: New quest progress value (0.0 to 1.0). Only set when meaningful progress occurs. Omit otherwise.

timeOfDay.description: New time of day if it changed. Omit if unchanged.

threatLevel.description: New threat level if the situation changed. Omit if unchanged.

suggestedActions.description: Two to three short suggested next actions for UI hints. Each should be a brief phrase the player could say, e.g. 'Explore the cave', 'Talk to the stranger', 'Draw your sword'.

shouldGenerateImage.description: True when the moment is visually significant: arriving at a new location, a dramatic reveal, a major battle beginning. Budget is 2 images per session.

imagePrompt.description: English-language scene description for the image generator. Required when shouldGenerateImage is true. Should describe the visual scene in detail suitable for DALL-E. Omit when shouldGenerateImage is false.

tutorialBeat.description: Current tutorial beat number (1 through 5). Only present when the session is a tutorial. Omit for normal game sessions. The backend uses this to track tutorial progress and trigger the next beat.
